turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some pretty bad stomach issues - crampy pain in my lower abdomen, mostly on the left side, and I'm having bowel movements 8 to 10 times a day. They're loose and watery, and it's really urgent.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The main symptom is definitely the diarrhea - it's like my body can't stop moving stuff out, and it's getting to the point where I'm worried about being in public or leaving my apartment because of the urgency. It's just really affecting my daily life.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 2 weeks ago, but I think it might have been a day or two after I took some antibiotics for a sinus infection. I'm not sure if that's related, though - it just seemed to come on out of nowhere and hasn't really gone away since then.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens every single day, at least 8-10 times a day. Sometimes it feels like I'm running to the bathroom nonstop, it's that frequent.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's a solid 9 out of 10 - it's debilitating and affects everything I do. I've been using loperamide for two weeks, but it doesn't really help with the frequency or urgency, just slows things down a bit, which is better than nothing, but still not great.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: The cramping is pretty bad, especially on the left side. It feels like a sharp, stabbing pain that comes and goes with each bowel movement.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It gets worse when I'm stressed or under pressure - like when I have to meet deadlines at work. The cramping and urgency seem to spike when I'm on my computer for long periods, which is pretty much all day since I'm a software engineer.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't really experience those symptoms. The main issue is just the diarrhea and cramping. Sometimes when I'm under a lot of stress, like during a long meeting at work, my heart might feel a bit racing, but that's about it.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: The cramping is mostly confined to the lower left abdomen, but sometimes it can radiate up to my lower back, just on the left side. It feels like a tightness or pressure that moves with me when I move around, but it's mainly in my abdomen.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any noticeable swelling in my legs or feet. To be honest, with this diarrhea issue going on, I've been too preoccupied to really notice if anything else is out of the ordinary down there, but no unusual swelling comes to mind.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking loperamide, Imodium, for the diarrhea. I've been using it for the past two weeks, like I mentioned earlier. It doesn't really help with the frequency or urgency, but it does slow things down a bit, which is something at least.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I don't think so - my dad has type 2 diabetes, but I'm not aware of anything like that in my family.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I haven't really had any bloodwork done in a while, no. To be honest, it's been over two years since I've seen a doctor or gotten any kind of medical testing. I'm not sure if that's relevant, but I just haven't made it a priority until now with this diarrhea issue going on.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. And no, I don't really drink a lot of alcohol either - work has been pretty stressful lately, so I've actually cut back on that too.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet and exercise habits have taken a bit of a hit with all this going on. With long work hours, mostly takeout, I haven't been eating regular meals like I used to. It's just easier to grab something quick when I'm stuck in front of my computer for 12 hours a day. And as for exercise, it's been basically non-existent - I've been too tired and stressed out to even think about working out.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, that's the only thing that comes to mind right now. I'm not aware of any heart-related issues in my family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, like I mentioned earlier. That's the only serious medical condition that comes to mind right now.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I've seen a doctor or gotten any kind of medical testing, which is probably why I'm here today dealing with this diarrhea issue. To be honest, it's not something I prioritized until now when my daily life started getting really affected by these symptoms.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so - I've been taking loperamide for two weeks without any issues.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: To be honest, with this diarrhea issue going on, I've also noticed that I've lost a bit of weight over the past three weeks - probably around 4 kg (9 lbs) - and I'm feeling really fatigued. It's affecting my work performance, especially since I have to meet deadlines for my software project at the startup where I work. The stress from not being able to manage my time effectively due to these symptoms is just adding to the problem.
